data.title: Iranian media: The Strait of Hormuz is now completely closed.
data.description: According to Iran's Press TV, the Strait of Hormuz has been completely closed, forcing oil tankers to return to port. (Jinshi)

data.title: NBA Regular Season: Hawks vs. Cavaliers Prediction Market Activity
data.description:
A recent prediction market event on Polymarket has seen significant activity regarding the NBA regular season game between the Atlanta Hawks and the Cleveland Cavaliers. According to Odaily, an account with a win rate exceeding 55% has invested $120,000 on the Hawks defeating the Cavaliers, with an opening price of 39¢.

The game commenced at 7 a.m. UTC+8 and is approaching halftime. The NBA regular season is nearing its conclusion, with the Hawks holding a record of 45 wins and 34 losses, placing them fifth in the Eastern Conference. Meanwhile, the Cavaliers have a record of 50 wins and 29 losses, ranking fourth in the Eastern Conference.

data.title: Federal Reserve Officials Consider Rate Adjustments Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Federal Reserve officials are evaluating various scenarios for the U.S. economy following the outbreak of war in Iran. According to PANews, the minutes from the March FOMC meeting, released on Wednesday, reveal that most officials are concerned about the potential impact of the conflict on the labor market, which may necessitate a reduction in interest rates. However, many officials also highlighted inflation risks, which could ultimately require rate hikes. The minutes indicate that an increasing number of officials recommend including statements in post-meeting communications about the possibility of rate increases under specific conditions. The minutes state, 'Some participants believe there is sufficient reason to describe future rate decisions in a two-way manner in the post-meeting statement, reflecting that raising the target range for rates may be appropriate if inflation remains above target levels.'

data.title: Non-Dollar Stablecoins and the Illusion of Currency Change
data.description: Non-dollar stablecoins often create the illusion that changing the unit of account equates to altering the currency system. According to PANews, the true value lies not in the label but in the infrastructure and control mechanisms behind it.

data.title: Foreign Investment in Japanese Stocks Surges
data.description: Foreign investors purchased Japanese stocks worth 29,596 billion yen in the week leading up to April 3, according to Jin10. This marks a significant turnaround from the previous week's figure of -44,481 billion yen, indicating renewed interest in Japan's equity market.

data.title: AI TRENDS | OpenAI's Enterprise Revenue Surges, Expected to Match Consumer Business by 2026
data.description: OpenAI's Chief Revenue Officer, Denise Dresser, announced that the company's enterprise business now accounts for over 40% of its total revenue. According to Jin10, this growth is built on the strong performance of its consumer business. Dresser projected that by the end of 2026, the enterprise segment is expected to equal the consumer business in revenue contribution. Additionally, OpenAI's Codex has recently surpassed 3 million weekly active users.

data.title: Crypto Card Usage Surges with $600 Million Monthly Volume in March
data.description: Crypto card transactions have seen a significant rise, reaching a monthly volume of $600 million in March. According to NS3.AI, this marks a substantial increase from $187 million recorded a year earlier. The growth indicates a broader adoption of crypto-linked debit and prepaid cards for point-of-sale transactions, bypassing traditional off-ramp systems. While USDT continues to dominate the card volume, USDC is gaining traction, particularly in Western markets.

data.title: Whale Addresses Holding Over 10 Million ADA Reach Four-Month High
data.description: The number of whale addresses holding more than 10 million ADA has increased to 424, marking the highest level in four months. According to NS3.AI, this count has been on the rise for the past nine weeks, indicating growing interest among large holders in the ADA market.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 71,000 USDT with a 0.81% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 08, 2026, 23:01 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 71,000 USDT and is now trading at 70,957.023438 USDT, with a narrowed 0.81% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: U.S. Lawmaker Urges SEC Investigation into Pre-Iran Strike Market Activity
data.description:
A U.S. lawmaker has called for an investigation into unusual trading activities in the oil and stock futures markets prior to U.S. President Donald Trump's decision to delay a military strike on Iran last month. According to BlockBeats, Representative Ritchie Torres expressed concerns over the suspicious nature of the trading, citing the speed, scale, and structure of the transactions.

Torres, a Democrat, emphasized the need for the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) to take action. He urged these regulatory bodies to conduct a formal investigation into the trading activities that occurred just minutes before Trump's March 23 post, and to obtain the trading records associated with these accounts.

data.title: Iran Revolutionary Guard Reports Disruption in Hormuz Strait Passage Due to Israel's Ceasefire Violation
data.description: On April 9, the Iranian Revolutionary Guard announced a disruption in the passage through the Hormuz Strait. According to BlockBeats, this interruption was attributed to Israel's violation of the ceasefire agreement in Lebanon. The Hormuz Strait is a critical maritime route, and any disruption can have significant implications for global trade and regional stability.

data.title: U.S. Vice President Criticizes Iranian Proposal, Suggests AI Involvement
data.description:
U.S. Vice President Vance has expressed skepticism regarding the first ten-point proposal from Iran, suggesting it might have been generated by ChatGPT. According to BlockBeats, this proposal was immediately discarded and rejected.

A second, more reasonable ten-point proposal has emerged, developed through consultations with Pakistan. This proposal, referenced by U.S. President Donald Trump, remains valid as of yesterday. Additionally, a third ten-point proposal, deemed more extreme than the first, has been circulating widely on social media platforms.

data.title: Iran Establishes Safe Passage Routes in Strait of Hormuz
data.description:
Iran has delineated secure passage routes in the Strait of Hormuz, according to BlockBeats. The Iranian Ports and Maritime Organization announced on Wednesday that vessels must coordinate with the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps to use these routes.

The designated safe entry route directs ships from the Sea of Oman to the north side of Larak Island. Conversely, the safe exit route from the Gulf requires passage via the south side of Larak Island into the Sea of Oman.

data.title: Iran Agrees to Transfer Enriched Uranium Stockpile, White House Confirms
data.description:
The White House has confirmed that Iran has agreed to transfer its enriched uranium stockpile, marking a significant development in U.S. President Donald Trump's efforts to prevent Iran from developing nuclear weapons. According to BlockBeats, White House Press Secretary Levitt stated on Wednesday that this issue is a top priority for President Trump, who has cited the prevention of Iran's nuclear weapon development as a key reason for potential military action.

Levitt emphasized during a briefing that this is a non-negotiable red line for the President, who is committed to ensuring the transfer takes place. When asked if Iran had indeed agreed to the transfer, Levitt confirmed it but did not provide further details.

International Atomic Energy Agency Director General Grossi noted that nearly half of Iran's enriched uranium, with enrichment levels reaching up to 60%, is stored in a tunnel facility in Isfahan. This facility was reportedly the only target not severely damaged during a U.S.-Israeli strike on Iran's nuclear sites in June of the previous year.

U.S. Defense Secretary Hegseth commented that the uranium is currently underground, and the situation is being closely monitored. He affirmed that the U.S. is fully aware of Iran's capabilities and retains the right to conduct further military strikes if necessary to destroy the enriched uranium.

data.title: The SEC appointed David Woodcock as head of enforcement, drawing attention to the direction of crypto enforcement.
data.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) announced the appointment of David Woodcock as the new head of its enforcement division, succeeding the outgoing director. Woodcock previously served as the SEC's Fort Worth office director (2011–2015), overseeing enforcement lawyers, reviewers, and accounting teams, and participating in investigations across virtually all major enforcement areas. He recently became a partner at the law firm Gibson Dunn. Notably, Woodcock lacks a clear background in the crypto industry, and this appointment comes at a time of controversy surrounding the SEC's approach to crypto enforcement. Previously, disagreements over case handling and enforcement standards led to the previous head's short tenure and drew congressional attention. SEC Chairman Paul Atkins stated that this appointment will help strengthen the fight against "misconduct that causes the greatest harm to investors."

data.title: Yuga Labs and Ryder Ripps reach settlement, ending BAYC trademark dispute.
data.description: Yuga Labs and artist Ryder Ripps have reached a settlement in a long-standing trademark dispute, marking a near end to the legal battle surrounding Bored Ape Yacht Club (BAYC). Under the settlement agreement, Ripps and his partners will be prohibited from continuing to use Yuga Labs' images and trademarks. Specific terms have not yet been fully disclosed. The case began in 2022 when Ripps launched a replica NFT series (RR/BAYC) using the same ape image as BAYC, calling it "expressive appropriation art." Yuga Labs subsequently filed a trademark infringement lawsuit. In 2023, a U.S. court ruled that the replica project constituted trademark infringement and initially awarded approximately $9 million in damages. An appeals court later partially overturned the ruling and ordered a retrial, leading to a protracted legal battle.

data.title: AI TRENDS | U.S. Court Allows Defense Department to Blacklist Anthropic
data.description: A U.S. court has decided not to prevent the Department of Defense from blacklisting Anthropic, a company known for its AI developments. According to Jin10, this decision allows the Defense Department to proceed with its plans to restrict Anthropic's access to certain government contracts and collaborations. The move comes amid increasing scrutiny of AI companies and their involvement in sensitive sectors. Anthropic, which has been at the forefront of AI innovation, faces challenges as it navigates the implications of this decision. The court's ruling highlights the ongoing tension between technological advancement and national security concerns.

data.title: Wall Street Journal: Trump Considers Withdrawing US Troops from Some NATO Allies
data.description: According to US government officials, the Trump administration is considering a plan to punish some NATO members it believes did not support the US and Israel during the war with Iran. The proposal includes withdrawing US troops from non-supporting NATO members and redeploying them to countries more supportive of US military operations. This proposal is far less serious than Trump's recent threats to completely withdraw the US from NATO, which he cannot do without congressional approval. The plan has been circulating and gaining support among senior government officials in recent weeks and is currently in its early conceptual stages, one of several options for punishing NATO being discussed by the White House. The White House previously stated that Trump believes NATO has failed the "test" and will discuss withdrawal with Rutte. According to two government officials, in addition to redeploying troops, the plan may also include closing US military bases in at least one European country, possibly Spain or Germany. Countries such as Poland, Romania, Lithuania, and Greece could benefit as they are seen as supporters. The plan could lead to a greater deployment of US troops closer to the Russian border, likely angering Moscow. (Wall Street Journal)

data.title: "The Fed's mouthpiece": Ceasefire agreement makes the Fed's decision more difficult.
data.description: Nick Timiraos, often referred to as the "Federal Reserve mouthpiece," wrote that the ceasefire between the US and Iran offers an opportunity to mitigate the serious threat currently posed to the global economy. However, for the Federal Reserve, this may simply be a change of scenery: persistent energy price volatility is enough to keep inflation high, but not enough to severely damage demand, thus prolonging the period of unchanged interest rates. The minutes of the Fed's March meeting emphasized that the war was not the primary reason for the Fed's reluctance to cut rates, but rather it complicated the Fed's already cautious stance. Even before the conflict, the path to rate cuts had narrowed. The labor market had stabilized, alleviating concerns about a recession, and progress toward the Fed's 2% inflation target had stalled. The Fed did not adjust interest rates at its March meeting, partly due to concerns about the risks of a protracted war. The risk that escalating conflict could drag down economic growth and lead to a recession was the last and strongest argument supporting a resumption of rate cuts. Paradoxically, however, the end of the war may, in the short term, make it more difficult, rather than easier, for the Fed to implement easing policies. This is because the ceasefire agreement eliminates the worst-case economic scenario, namely severe price increases that would disrupt supply chains and destroy demand, which is arguably more important than eliminating the risk of new inflationary pressures.

data.title: Trump Administration Considers NATO Troop Redeployment Plan
data.description:
The Trump administration is contemplating a plan to penalize certain NATO member countries that U.S. President Donald Trump believes did not support the United States and Israel during the Iran conflict. According to Odaily, the proposal includes withdrawing U.S. troops from NATO countries that did not provide support and redeploying them to nations more aligned with U.S. military actions.

This proposal is less severe than Trump's recent threats to completely withdraw the United States from NATO, a move he cannot make without congressional approval. The plan has been circulating among senior government officials in recent weeks and has garnered some support. It remains in the preliminary conceptual stage and is one of several punitive measures against NATO being discussed by the White House.

The White House has previously stated that Trump believes NATO has not passed the "test" and plans to discuss "withdrawal" with Dutch Prime Minister Mark Rutte. According to two government officials, the plan might also involve closing at least one U.S. military base in Europe, potentially in Spain or Germany. Countries like Poland, Romania, Lithuania, and Greece could benefit, as they are viewed as supporters.

The plan could result in more U.S. troops being stationed closer to the Russian border, a move likely to provoke Moscow.
